page.content: In analyzing Texas Instruments' performance within the semiconductor and electronics sectors, the accompanying table offers an insightful overview of the geographic revenue mix and foundry capacity utilization across key regions. Notably, North America's year-over-year change (YoYChange(%)) was an impressive 500%, reflecting robust demand and operational efficiencies that have positioned the region as a leader in the market. In contrast, Europe and Asia-Pacific exhibited YoYChange(%) figures of 300% and 200%, respectively, indicating varying levels of growth potential within these markets.

lines.text: In analyzing Texas Instruments' performance within the semiconductor and electronics sectors, the accompanying table offers
an insightful overview of the geographic revenue mix and foundry capacity utilization across key regions. Notably, North
America's year-over-year change (YoYChange(%)) was an impressive 500%, reflecting robust demand and operational
efficiencies that have positioned the region as a leader in the market. In contrast, Europe and Asia-Pacific exhibited
YoYChange(%) figures of 300% and 200%, respectively, indicating varying levels of growth potential within these markets.

page.content: The total market share for Texas Instruments reached 15.5%, underscoring its competitive stance in the industry.

lines.text: The total market share for Texas Instruments reached 15.5%, underscoring its competitive stance in the industry.

page.content: Moreover, the data reveals that analog chips accounted for a substantial portion of the revenue, with digital chips generating $45M, contributing to a capacity utilization rate of 85%. This high utilization reflects a strategic focus on optimizing production capabilities, which is essential for meeting the growing demand for advanced semiconductor solutions. As the company continues to innovate, the year-over-year increase of +2% in overall capacity utilization demonstrates its commitment to operational excellence, positioning Texas Instruments favorably for future growth in the dynamic landscape of semiconductors.
